source_pr.title: privilege/privileges:  refactor the data struct for user privilege data
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #55563

Problem Summary:

### What changed and how does it work?

The old data struct is not fit for a lot of users, it use copy-on-write and, when there are 2M users, the cost of copy 2M item for every change is not acceptable.

I have optimize the code to avoid holding all user's privilege data in-memory, and instead maintain just the active users.
However, it does not help the worst case when all users are active.

So this commit is for the worst case:  refactor the data struct using a btree, make the update cost small enough.

The main difference is the `merge` function.
Before this change, we merge two (huge) slices s1, s2:  create a new big slice s3,  copy the data from the old one s1, remove duplicated, and than append the data from s2.

Now it works by s1.Clone(), and updates on this clone by adding the data of s2. 
The underlying btree implement is a lazy clone,  the clone will share the same node with the old btree, and copy-on-write after the modifications.  This way the cost is much smaller.


### Check List

- [ ] No need to test

  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it depended on an implicit ordering of user privilege records. After refactoring the in-memory privilege cache structure, the iteration/return order of records is not guaranteed to match the previous behavior. The fix sorts the returned UserRecord slice using a stable comparator before assertions/comparisons.
fix_pattern: Sort the returned privilege/user record slices (stable comparator) before comparing in tests to avoid nondeterministic iteration order.
